content:
If this task is complete  I want you to show me deploy and verified  you no need to do again stop wasting my time # IVX FINAL BLOCKER CLOSURE — COMPLETE ITEMS 1 THROUGH 10 ONE BY ONE

MODE: EXECUTE NOW
PRIORITY: P0
GOAL: Close every remaining blocker from the final 16-phase QA audit, deploy all fixes, and return live proof.

IMPORTANT RULES:

- Complete items strictly in numerical order.
- Do not skip ahead.
- Do not group multiple items into one vague update.
- After each item, return:
  1. files inspected
  2. files changed
  3. commands run
  4. exit codes
  5. test result
  6. commit status
  7. deployment status
  8. PASS / FAIL
- Do not mark an item complete unless the exact acceptance criteria pass.
- Do not mark final VERIFIED until all items pass.
- Do not expose secrets.
- Do not modify investor, buyer, member, deal, payment, or authentication records unless explicitly required and owner-approved.
- Preserve business-data counts before and after.

==================================================
ITEM 1 — PUSH THE THREE LOCAL FIXES
==================================================

Current local fixes reported:

1. `/api/ivx/deploy` missing owner authentication
2. `/api/ivx/investors` returning fallback instead of paginated data
3. corrupted values in `expo/.env`

Required work:

- inspect the exact diff
- verify no unrelated files are included
- run lint
- run typecheck
- run targeted tests
- commit the three fixes
- push to `main`
- confirm remote SHA

Acceptance criteria:

- GitHub commit exists
- remote SHA equals local SHA
- no unrelated changes
- all targeted tests pass

Return:

ITEM 1 STATUS: PASS / FAIL

==================================================
ITEM 2 — DEPLOY ITEM 1 TO RENDER
==================================================

After Item 1 passes:

- trigger Render deployment
- capture deployment ID
- wait for completion
- verify deployed SHA equals GitHub SHA
- verify `/health`
- verify `/readiness`
- verify boot time

Acceptance criteria:

- deployment completed successfully
- deployed SHA matches GitHub
- health 200
- readiness 200

Return:

ITEM 2 STATUS: PASS / FAIL

==================================================
ITEM 3 — VERIFY DEPLOY ROUTE SECURITY
==================================================

Test `/api/ivx/deploy` with:

1. no token
2. invalid token
3. non-owner token
4. valid owner token

Expected:

- no token → 401
- invalid token → 401 or 403
- non-owner → 403
- valid owner → allowed only under existing approval rules

Also verify:

- no public user can trigger deployment
- no anonymous route exists
- no fallback bypass exists
- request is logged with trace ID
- no secrets appear in logs

Acceptance criteria:

- unauthorized deployment is impossible
- owner-only route protection works in production

Return:

ITEM 3 STATUS: PASS / FAIL

==================================================
ITEM 4 — VERIFY INVESTORS PAGINATION
==================================================

Test `/api/ivx/investors` in production.

Run:

- default request
- `limit=5`
- `limit=50`
- `limit=200`
- `limit=500`
- invalid negative limit
- limit above 500
- page/cursor continuation
- empty result behavior
- owner access
- unauthorized access

Expected:

- default returns real investor rows
- no fallback placeholder
- `limit=5` returns 5 or fewer real records
- max limit enforced at 500
- pagination metadata included
- no duplicate rows across pages
- no missing rows caused by pagination
- correct total or cursor metadata
- stable sorting
- HTTP status correct
- investor records remain unchanged

Acceptance criteria:

- real paginated data returned
- no fallback response
- no data duplication or mutation

Return:

ITEM 4 STATUS: PASS / FAIL

==================================================
ITEM 5 — FIX BUYER DISCOVERY NULL RESPONSE
==================================================

Current bug:

`/api/ivx/buyer-discovery` returns:

`buyers: null`

Required:

- inspect route
- inspect query
- inspect data mapping
- inspect empty-state behavior
- inspect RLS
- inspect source table/view
- inspect serialization

Correct behavior:

- return `buyers: []` when no buyers exist
- return real buyer objects when records exist
- never return `null`
- include pagination metadata if applicable
- preserve correct authorization
- add regression tests

Test:

- no buyers
- one buyer
- multiple buyers
- unauthorized
- invalid filter
- empty search
- pagination

Acceptance criteria:

- `buyers` is always an array
- route works in production
- tests pass

Commit, push, deploy, and verify before moving on.

Return:

ITEM 5 STATUS: PASS / FAIL

==================================================
ITEM 6 — REMOVE ALL REMAINING RORK SDK RUNTIME DEPENDENCY
==================================================

Current reported findings:

- 5 Rork SDK references
- `@rork-ai/toolkit-sdk`
- `withRorkMetro`
- 5 Rork public variables in `.env`

Audit all repository references for:

- `@rork-ai/toolkit-sdk`
- `withRorkMetro`
- `rork`
- `RORK_`
- `EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK_`
- Rork domains
- Rork API URLs
- Rork auth URLs
- Rork functions URLs
- Rork toolkit secret
- Rork app key
- Rork fallback provider
- Rork proxy
- Rork build plugin

For each reference return:

- file
- line
- purpose
- active at runtime yes/no
- replacement
- removal status

Required work:

- remove unused Rork packages
- remove Rork imports
- remove Rork Metro wrapper
- remove Rork runtime fallback
- remove Rork public variables
- remove any Rork network calls
- replace required functionality with owner-controlled services
- preserve app build functionality

Security requirement:

No secret may exist under `EXPO_PUBLIC_*`.

Acceptance criteria:

- zero runtime Rork imports
- zero runtime Rork network calls
- zero Rork public secrets
- app builds successfully
- Owner AI still works
- GitHub/Render/Supabase still work
- no feature regression

Commit, push, deploy, rebuild app, verify.

Return:

ITEM 6 STATUS: PASS / FAIL

==================================================
ITEM 7 — RECONCILE PROJECT VIDEO COUNT
==================================================

Current discrepancy:

- one report shows 8
- another shows 17

Audit:

- physical table rows
- active rows
- archived rows
- soft-deleted rows
- duplicate rows
- media associations
- project associations
- API count
- UI count
- landing count
- reels count

Return exact reconciliation:

- physical total
- active total
- visible total
- API total
- UI total
- reason for 8 vs 17

Do not delete duplicates automatically.

Acceptance criteria:

- one documented canonical count
- database/API/UI variance explained or fixed
- no unauthorized deletion

Return:

ITEM 7 STATUS: PASS / FAIL

==================================================
ITEM 8 — FIX BACKEND PACKAGE STRUCTURE
==================================================

Current pre-existing issue:

`@supabase/supabase-js` fails to resolve in clean local backend tests because the backend has no correct package dependency structure.

Required:

- inspect root `package.json`
- inspect backend `package.json`
- inspect workspaces
- inspect lockfile
- inspect Bun configuration
- inspect tsconfig
- inspect test runner module resolution

Fix so:

- backend declares required production dependencies
- clean install works
- tests do not depend on accidental root resolution
- Supabase module resolves from backend context
- lockfile is consistent
- no duplicate incompatible versions

Run from clean state:

- remove install artifacts if safe
- locked install
- dependency tree
- backend tests
- full tests
- typecheck
- production build

Acceptance criteria:

- 260/260 tests execute
- no Supabase module-resolution failure
- no hidden dependency fallback
- production build passes

Commit, push, deploy if runtime files changed.

Return:

ITEM 8 STATUS: PASS / FAIL

==================================================
ITEM 9 — REAL DEVICE MOBILE QA
==================================================

Build or use the latest production APK.

Test on real Android device:

1. clean install
2. cold launch
3. owner login
4. logout
5. login again
6. app kill/reopen
7. session persistence
8. Home
9. 3 regular investment cards first
10. up to 8 images per regular card
11. Casa Rosario reel after card 3
12. Reels module sizing
13. Deal Detail
14. View Deal
15. Invest Now
16. Owner AI normal chat
17. Owner AI developer task
18. image upload
19. PDF upload
20. message persistence
21. Members
22. Investors
23. Buyers
24. CRM
25. Android back button
26. slow network
27. reconnect
28. no crash
29. no infinite loading
30. no diagnostic banner

Acceptance criteria:

- all 30 tests pass
- screenshots or video evidence attached
- device model and Android version reported

Return:

ITEM 9 STATUS: PASS / FAIL

==================================================
ITEM 10 — FINAL PRODUCTION REGRESSION
==================================================

After Items 1–9 pass, run final production regression.

Verify:

- GitHub SHA
- Render SHA
- health SHA
- all three match
- landing live
- chat live
- Owner AI provider ready
- Owner AI message success
- senior-developer task success
- Live Work real events
- attachments
- Members
- Investors
- Buyers
- CRM
- deals
- reels
- View Deal
- Invest Now
- autonomous scheduler
- push notification configuration
- no Rork runtime dependency
- no public secret
- no unauthorized deploy route
- all backend tests pass
- no P0/P1 issue remains

Record business-data safety:

- members before/after
- investors before/after
- buyers before/after
- deals before/after
- project videos before/after

Expected unauthorized changes:

ZERO

==================================================
MANDATORY ITEM-BY-ITEM RESPONSE FORMAT
==================================================

For each item use this exact format:

ITEM [NUMBER]:
STATUS: PASS / FAIL / BLOCKED

FILES INSPECTED:
-

FILES CHANGED:
-

COMMANDS RUN:
1. Command:
   Exit code:
   Result:

TESTS:
-

GITHUB:
- Commit SHA:
- Remote SHA:

DEPLOYMENT:
- Deploy ID:
- Deployed SHA:
- Health:
- Readiness:

LIVE PROOF:
-

BLOCKERS:
- NONE or exact blocker

Do not move to the next item until the current item is PASS.

==================================================
FINAL REPORT
==================================================

ITEM 1 — Push three local fixes:
PASS / FAIL

ITEM 2 — Render deployment:
PASS / FAIL

ITEM 3 — Deploy route security:
PASS / FAIL

ITEM 4 — Investors pagination:
PASS / FAIL

PASS / FAIL

PASS / FAIL

PASS / FAIL

PASS / FAIL

PASS / FAIL

ITEM 10 — Final production regression:
PASS / FAIL

GITHUB PROOF:
- Repository:

DEPLOYMENT PROOF:

- Members before/after:

REMAINING ISSUES:

FINAL VERIFIED RULE:

Use VERIFIED only if Items 1 through 10 are all PASS.

No “mostly verified.”
No “verified with pending QA.”
No “local fix complete.”
No “deployment pending.”
No skipped items.
